text: Dry cough started approximately 5-6 days ago, beginning after the holiday
transcript_quote: Five days. Maybe six. A cough first. Dry.

text: Fever developed after cough onset and has persisted; feverish by New Year's Eve
transcript_quote: Then the fever came... and stayed.

text: Progressive dyspnea; this morning could not walk from bed to bathroom without stopping twice to catch her breath
transcript_quote: this morning she could not get from the bed to the bathroom without stopping twice to breathe

text: Chest tightness described as 'air is thin,' without pain
transcript_quote: No pain. Just tight. Like the air is... thin.

text: Decreased oral intake — stopped eating properly and barely drank for two days
transcript_quote: She barely drank anything for two days. I kept trying.

text: Mild confusion/'foggy' this morning, but oriented and interactive
transcript_quote: She seemed a little foggy this morning, but as you can hear, she is still herself.

text: COVID-19 confirmed: positive swab at urgent care and repeat positive swab in the ED
transcript_quote: We repeated the swab here in the emergency department and it is positive as well, so the diagnosis is confirmed.

text: Denies chest pain or pressure (patient recognizes it differs from her prior heart attack)
transcript_quote: This is not that. No pain.

text: Denies hemoptysis and leg swelling
transcript_quote: Any coughing up blood? Leg swelling? PT: No. No.

text: Denies falls, fainting
transcript_quote: No falls.

text: Tree nut allergy (walnut) with prior significant swelling; flagged to dietary and on armband
transcript_quote: the allergy — tree nuts... She swelled up badly once from a walnut brownie.

text: Home medication: aspirin 81 mg daily
transcript_quote: aspirin eighty-one milligrams daily

text: Home medication: atenolol 50 mg
transcript_quote: atenolol fifty

text: Home medication: rosuvastatin 40 mg
transcript_quote: rosuvastatin forty

text: Home medication: lisinopril 20 mg
transcript_quote: lisinopril twenty

text: Past medical history includes hypertension
transcript_quote: I have high blood pressure

text: History of ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) years ago
transcript_quote: the heart attack we mentioned — an ST-elevation heart attack some years back

text: Past medical history includes hyperlipidemia
transcript_quote: high cholesterol

text: Past medical history includes metabolic syndrome and prediabetes
transcript_quote: metabolic syndrome, prediabetes

text: Past medical history includes osteoporosis
transcript_quote: osteoporosis

text: Past medical history includes obesity
transcript_quote: obesity on the problem list

text: History of scoliosis since adolescence
transcript_quote: scoliosis from adolescence

text: Admission diagnosis of pneumonia with viral lung inflammation on chest x-ray
transcript_quote: The chest x-ray we took shows pneumonia — the virus has inflamed the lungs themselves.

text: Hypoxemia present
transcript_quote: your oxygen level was about eighty-seven percent

text: Respiratory distress present
Arrival oxygen saturation 87% on room air
transcript_quote: your oxygen level was about eighty-seven percent

text: Arrival temperature 38.5°C
transcript_quote: your temperature was thirty-eight and a half

text: Arrival heart rate in the 50s (bradycardia)
transcript_quote: your heart rate was in the fifties

text: Arrival blood pressure 105/53 mmHg
transcript_quote: blood pressure one-oh-five over fifty-three

text: Oxygen saturation dropped to ~76% lying flat with fever climbing toward 39°C and heart rate rising to high 80s
transcript_quote: her oxygen dropped to about seventy-six percent lying flat, her fever is climbing toward thirty-nine, and her heart rate has come up to the high eighties

text: Leukopenia — WBC low (~3.4 x10^3/uL)
transcript_quote: Her white blood cell count is a little low

text: Thrombocytopenia — platelets low (~136 x10^3/uL)
transcript_quote: Her platelets are on the lower side as well, which we will follow.

text: Elevated creatinine at 2.7 mg/dL with acute kidney strain
transcript_quote: the creatinine is elevated at two point seven

text: Markedly reduced estimated GFR (~11 mL/min)
transcript_quote: the filtration estimate is quite low

text: Mild hyperkalemia — potassium 5.07 mmol/L
transcript_quote: Her potassium is also running slightly high, which goes along with the kidney strain

text: Chest x-ray performed, showing pneumonia
transcript_quote: The chest x-ray we took shows pneumonia

text: Admission to hospital in an isolation room due to confirmed COVID and pneumonia
transcript_quote: That combination is why you are being admitted, and because of the virus it has to be an isolation room.

text: Continuous oxygen administration by mask, flow titrated to maintain safe saturation
transcript_quote: First, oxygen by mask, continuously, and we adjust the flow to keep your levels safe.

text: Prone positioning sessions daily as tolerated, with padding for scoliosis and scheduled rotation
transcript_quote: we are going to have you spend a good part of each day lying on your stomach. It is called prone positioning.

text: Order for daily morning labs: CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, troponin (given cardiac history), coagulation studies, and iron studies

slots.tests: CBC, CMP, troponin, coagulation, iron studies

transcript_quote: blood work, every morning. A blood count, a chemistry panel, and on a regular schedule some extras — a heart enzyme called troponin... clotting tests... and iron studies.

text: Plan to cautiously adjust/hold blood pressure medications while kidneys recover, guided by daily labs
transcript_quote: we will be careful and deliberate with her blood pressure medicines while the kidneys recover

text: Plan for gentle IV/oral fluids and monitoring for dehydration and renal strain
transcript_quote: Gentle fluids, monitoring, and time.

text: Counseled patient to immediately use call button for worsening breathing, mask feeling inadequate, chest pain, or palpitations
transcript_quote: If breathing gets harder, if the mask feels like it is not enough, if you feel chest pain or your heart racing — press the call button immediately.

text: Counseled family that condition is serious given age and comorbidities, will be watched closely
transcript_quote: Honest answer: this is serious, and I am worried enough to watch her very closely.

text: Risk of infection triggering another cardiac event discussed; troponin monitored and aspirin/cardiac meds continued as labs allow
transcript_quote: It is a real risk with severe infections, which is exactly why we are checking the troponin regularly and keeping her on her aspirin and heart medicines as long as the labs allow.

text: Isolation precautions: no in-room visitors; daily video calls arranged and physician to call daughter each evening with updates

slots.plan: daily video call and evening physician call to daughter

transcript_quote: The nurses will help you two video call every day, and Teresa, I will call you personally with updates

text: Tree nut allergy communicated to dietary/kitchen for meal safety
transcript_quote: Tree nut allergy, flagging it for dietary right now, and it is on her armband.

text: Daughter (Teresa) present as caregiver and support, actively involved in history and care coordination
transcript_quote: Teresa, her daughter. They let me gown up to help get her settled.

text: Oxygen saturation improved to low 90s on oxygen mask after application
transcript_quote: you are holding in the low nineties on the mask
